fields.description:
# Comprehensive live reconciler validation + guaranteed bilateral cleanup

## Why
Prove the reconciler correct end-to-end against live Jira. An agent with ACLI access runs the full bidirectional
matrix and GUARANTEES cleanup in both systems. Validates POST-FIX behavior, so it runs AFTER the C-band lands
(several signals are the TARGET behavior those tickets create). Ordering captured here, not as hard depends_on
edges (avoids blocking-link promotion across mixed-type siblings); harness build may start early.

## Signal definitions (each an observable pass criterion)
- C1 (conflict signal): assert against the exact conflict record C1 (feline-wandering-dassierat) lands — reference
  C1's implemented signal at validation time (C1 is pre-manifest; do NOT hard-code an unverified field).
- C2 (hard-delete -> re-create): after a Jira hard-delete, a NEW issue with the same `rebar-id:<local_id>` label +
  entity property is created and bound; no false comment (lowerclass-creased-fruitbat).
- C3 (field-allowlist-drop alert): the deduped BRIDGE_ALERT C3 (nonglacial-tense-vulture) ADDS fires on a dropped
  outbound field (today only stderr — assert the post-C3 alert).
- C4 (429/backoff): monkey-patch the LIVE retry path C4 (lambswool-shaded-eider) implements — the `_run_acli`
  subprocess retry loop and/or `_call_with_retry` (dispatch_one.py) — NOT the dead `_call_with_backoff`. Because a
  subprocess `Retry-After` may be unavailable (per C4's probe), assert the JITTERED-BACKOFF behavior C4 lands (a
  simulated 429 exit -> backoff -> success), and Retry-After honoring only IF C4 confirmed availability.
- Echo suppression: outbound comments carry `<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->`; field/status writes are baseline-seeded
  via the `ProvenanceLedger` (defined in `conflict_resolver.py`) — verify `mutation_count==0` on the next pass over
  just-written data (harness pre-seeds the baseline as a real pass would).
- Status round-trip incl. LOSSY labels: local `blocked` -> Jira nearest live status + `rebar-status:blocked` label
  -> inbound restores `blocked`; `idea<->IDEA` round-trips; assert no oscillation over N=3 passes.
- TOMBSTONE/GRACE: local archive -> Jira Done (not deleted); a Jira 404 on N=`RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE`
  (default 3) consecutive passes -> binding soft-retired to `bindings-retired.json`; a single 404 does NOT retire.
- Blast-radius breaker: with >=11 synthetic bindings, one acting decision (fraction 0.09) does NOT trip; a synthetic
  mass-change trips it. Assert via the breaker API in `classify.py`: `census()['breaker']['allowed'] == False`
  (`BreakerVerdict.allowed`; both live in `classify.py`, NOT binding_walk.py). NOTE `census()` adds the `'breaker'`
  key only when the breaker is evaluated on an acting pass — the mass-change scenario provides that.

## Harness (guaranteed bilateral teardown)
- Build on the existing `e2e_validation_probe.sh` if suitable; else a pytest autouse fixture `yield`+`finally`
  (or bash `trap cleanup EXIT`) that hard-deletes Jira test issues AND discards the local throwaway env — runs even
  on assertion failure/exception, and tracks WHICH of N synthetic artifacts were created (partial-setup) so teardown
  removes exactly those.
- Teardown-failure fallback: each cleanup call retries 3x with exponential backoff; on exhaustion append the id to
  `leaked-artifacts.log` (a CI artifact) and exit non-zero.
- Setup probe: create one throwaway issue and assert `client.delete_issue` returns `{"status":"deleted"}`; NOTE
  `acli.py:721-723` RAISES PermissionError on missing delete perm (fails loud), so a scope misconfig surfaces here.

## Isolation, credentials
- Dedicated Jira TEST project; a service account with "Delete Issues" scoped to that project only; token via a
  secret, never logged/committed. Local side: an ISOLATED throwaway tickets env/branch discarded post-run.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Harness with GUARANTEED bilateral teardown (tracks partial-setup); a teardown-under-failure test injects a RuntimeError after create and asserts BOTH artifacts cleaned.
- [ ] One observable pass criterion per matrix scenario (C1-C4, echo, status-round-trip incl. lossy labels, tombstone/grace, idempotency mutation_count==0, blast-radius via `classify.census()['breaker']['allowed']==False`); harness exits 0 all-green and writes a JSON/JUnit report to a named path attached to the ticket.
- [ ] Teardown-failure fallback (3x exp-backoff, `leaked-artifacts.log` CI artifact, non-zero exit); ZERO surviving artifacts on success.
- [ ] Delete-permission probe passes (delete_issue returns deleted; raises loud if unscoped); credential scoped to the test project + injected via secret.
- [ ] Isolation documented; production REB + tickets branch untouched; N (oscillation passes) = 3.
- [ ] Runs after the C-band lands; re-runnable (idempotent teardown); harness pattern recorded in an ADR/design note.
